summary.intent: Fix issues in an E2E test file (mcp.spec.js) across multiple review iterations using Greptile automated code review.
summary.outcome: Completed iteration 2 of 5, fixing a test assertion to use `toHaveText` (with auto-retry) instead of a one-shot `textContent()` read, and pushed the changes.

repo: Project uses Greptile for automated PR code review with an iterative fix loop (up to 5 iterations)

code.finding: Prefer `toHaveText()` over `textContent()` in Playwright assertions — `toHaveText` has built-in auto-retry, making tests more resilient to async rendering

workflow: Greptile review cycle: push → trigger review → poll for completion → fetch comments → fix → resolve thread → commit → repeat
Unresolved Greptile threads must be explicitly resolved via API/CLI after addressing the feedback

friction: Multi-step polling loop required to wait for Greptile review completion (trigger → poll → fetch) adds latency to each iteration
open_items: Iterations 3–5 still pending — more Greptile feedback may surface additional fixes needed in mcp.spec.js
